content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be redesigned to mimic the prefrontal-hippocampal interaction that governs biological memory retrieval, replacing static retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) knowledge injection with curiosity-driven active memory pruning and updating that aligns RAG with the agent's ongoing learning goals. This hybrid architecture would only retrieve external knowledge when the agent's existing active memory detects sufficient uncertainty, rather than pulling context for every generation task.
